message: Windows finalize does not provide the no-clobber guarantee: os.Rename on Windows replaces an existing destination file (documented os.Rename semantics since Go 1.5, implemented via MoveFileEx with MOVEFILE_REPLACE_EXISTING). The comment claiming rename 'refuses to replace an existing destination' is false, so the concurrent-window overwrite this contract fixes remains open on Windows, failing acceptance criterion 2. The new test TestExportFinalizeRefusesToReplaceOutputCreatedAfterStaging (internal/app/export_test.go:224) has no Windows skip and would fail on Windows; CI is ubuntu-only so it is never exercised. A no-replace Windows finalize needs MoveFileEx without the replace flag (golang.org/x/sys/windows) or an O_CREATE|O_EXCL create-and-copy.

Freshness pin is computed from a second read of the five review artifacts (memory.go:365) after the candidate content was built from earlier reads (memory.go:329-344). A review mutation landing between the reads yields a candidate with pre-mutation content and a post-mutation pin; memory accept then verifies the pin and accepts a candidate that does not match the review state it embeds. Hashing the same bytes parsed for candidate construction would close the window.

Hard-link finalize fails on filesystems without hard-link support (exFAT, FAT32, some SMB/NFS mounts): os.Link returns EPERM/ENOTSUP, so exporting to e.g. an exFAT external drive — which previously succeeded via rename — now always fails, and with a raw LinkError rather than a clear message since only EEXIST is mapped at export.go:322. An O_CREATE|O_EXCL create-and-copy fallback would preserve the no-replace guarantee on such destinations.

Windows finalize does not preserve no-clobber: Go's os.Rename replaces an existing destination on Windows (documented stdlib behavior since Go 1.5, golang/go#8914, MoveFileEx with MOVEFILE_REPLACE_EXISTING; repo pins go 1.26). The new comment asserts the opposite, acceptance criterion #2's Windows clause is unmet, and TestExportFinalizeRefusesToReplaceOutputCreatedAfterStaging would fail on GOOS=windows — unobserved because CI is ubuntu-only and never compiles or runs the Windows path. Minimal fix: use os.Link on Windows too (NTFS hard links fail with ERROR_ALREADY_EXISTS, recognized by os.IsExist).

nextCommandForStatus still returns 'pactum memory accept' unconditionally for memory_proposed, with no staleness check. It surfaces as NextCommand in task show (printed to humans under a 'Next:' header), task list, and status, so a stale-candidate run still advertises memory accept on those surfaces despite the in-scope item 'stale memory candidates do not advertise pactum memory accept'. The JSON next affordance (nextCommandsForRun) is correctly fixed and tested; accept itself refuses stale candidates safely with corrective affordances, and this selector is coarse by pre-existing design for other states (e.g. review_approved ignores pending proposals), so this is advisory.

Windows finalize path is never compiled or executed by any validation (gate ran on darwin, CI is ubuntu-only, make check has no GOOS=windows build), and the guarantee it encodes is false: Go documents os.Rename as replacing an existing non-directory newpath, implemented on Windows via MoveFileEx(MOVEFILE_REPLACE_EXISTING) since Go 1.5. TestExportFinalizeRefusesToReplaceOutputCreatedAfterStaging would fail if run on Windows but never runs there, so the suite reads green on acceptance criterion 2 while the Windows no-clobber behavior it asserts is unmet.

The directory call site of the new backslash validation (entry names built as archiveRoot+rel+"/") has no test; TestExportRejectsBackslashInEntryName covers only a regular file with a backslash, leaving the dir branch of collectExportEntries unexercised.

Successful exports now rely solely on the deferred os.Remove to clean up the staged archive (link-based finalize leaves the source name behind, unlike the old rename), and no success-path test asserts absence of .pactum-export-* leftovers; all assertNoExportLeftovers calls are in failure tests, so dropping the defer would pass the full suite.

docs/memory.md 'Propose, show, accept' (and the memory summary in docs/flow.md:284-289) does not document the new freshness-pin behavior: memory propose now writes a freshness_pin into memory-candidate.json, and memory accept verifies it and refuses stale candidates with a memory_candidate_stale error plus a context-sensitive fix (pactum memory propose <run_id>) or next (pactum review show <run_id>) affordance. In particular, candidates proposed before this change have no recognized pin and are now unconditionally rejected until re-proposed — a user-visible behavior break the docs should state.

docs/flow.md still documents export finalization as 'created via a temporary sibling file and an atomic rename', but the change replaced rename with a platform no-replace finalize: os.Link plus staged-file cleanup on Unix, rename only on Windows. The documented mechanism no longer matches the implementation or the strengthened no-clobber guarantee.

docs/flow.md's enumerated export failure conditions omit the new backslash rejection: export now fails — before producing the archive — when a run-record path's entry name still contains a literal backslash after filepath.ToSlash, naming the offending run-relative path. The failure list (output exists, parent missing, path inside run dir, unreadable file, malformed ledger, non-regular entries) is now incomplete.

Stale memory candidates are still advertised through the legacy next_command path. nextCommandsForRun is freshness-aware, but nextCommandForStatus returns `pactum memory accept` for every `memory_proposed` run, and task/status outputs still expose that value.

Missing test for unrecognized freshness-pin schemas. The contract requires missing, unrecognized, and mismatched freshness pins to be rejected, but the test only deletes the pin entirely; it does not cover a wrong schema with an otherwise valid hash.
The export workflow docs still say the archive is created via an atomic rename and omit the new failure case for run-record paths whose ZIP entry name contains a literal backslash.
The memory workflow docs do not mention that memory candidates are pinned to the current review state and that accept rejects stale or unpinned candidates until the user reruns memory propose.
